introSection: Pony Express is a Zamperla motorbike roller coaster located at Knott's Berry Farm in Buena Park, California, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: In late 2007, Knott's Berry Farm announced the addition of Pony Express. The ride opened on May 22, 2008.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Upon its opening, Pony Express had orange track and brown supports. In November 2021, it was repainted entirely brown.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 2 trains with 8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 16 riders per train.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On October 7, 2010, one of the trains failed to make it up the initial incline and rolled back, colliding with and damaging the other train in the station. Ten people were injured, receiving minor back and neck injuries. The accident was reportedly due to the brake fins being painted over, which prevented them from stopping the train. Pony Express reopened on October 18 after investigations. The park now tests the safety brakes when inspecting the ride.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Ride page on the park's website

fullText:
Pony Express is a Zamperla motorbike roller coaster located at Knott's Berry Farm in Buena Park, California, USA.

In late 2007, Knott's Berry Farm announced the addition of Pony Express. The ride opened on May 22, 2008.



Upon its opening, Pony Express had orange track and brown supports. In November 2021, it was repainted entirely brown.

2 trains with 8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 16 riders per train.

On October 7, 2010, one of the trains failed to make it up the initial incline and rolled back, colliding with and damaging the other train in the station. Ten people were injured, receiving minor back and neck injuries. The accident was reportedly due to the brake fins being painted over, which prevented them from stopping the train. Pony Express reopened on October 18 after investigations. The park now tests the safety brakes when inspecting the ride.




 * Ride page on the park's website
